transcript.text: This meeting is being recorded.
Hello.
Hey, how's going?
It's going well.
How you doing?
Busy.
As you can imagine, too.
But happy Friday.
Hopefully it's not too late.
Your time?
No, it's not.
That's 3.30 for me.
Okay.
It's not too bad.
You're in Nicaragua?
That's correct.
Nice.
Some of the best coffee.
feel like Nicaragua has a really good coffee, too.
Yeah, we do have a lot of coffee down here.
Yeah.
That's the main thing we actually export to other countries.
Yeah.
Well, great.
Great to connect.
Love to get a little bit of intro into a little bit of your journey background and kind of what you do with your interests.
You know, like we're trying to move fast as well in our end, but always like to start with a little bit of your journey, too.
Yeah, of course.
Well, first of all, thank you so much for the time and for the opportunity to consider me for this role.
So, a little bit about me.
I've been in recruiting for a little bit over 10 years now.
I started my journey here in Nicaragua working for call centers.
So, I was, you know, doing tech support, sales, service that was back into days, right?
But then I had the opportunity to essentially join one call center that had a recruiting campaign.
So, we were doing, we were supporting career builder back in days.
So, we, so that's where I started my journey in recruiting.
And so, I started like doing theory basics, like just reviewing resumes and whatnot.
And then I, I
After I got some experience doing that, then I was given the opportunity to start interviewing people.
so that's where I started interviewing people.
And then after about a year and a half, I had the opportunity to, essentially, my boss, who was the team lead, actually got promoted into an operations manager.
And so HACE's spot was available.
And essentially, I applied for the job and then got the opportunity.
So that's where my recruiting plus leadership journey started.
so I was essentially responsible for overseeing 25 recruiters.
Not all of them were doing like full cycle.
Some of them were just you and Rasmus, like I did at the beginning.
But it was a pretty large team.
So I had to essentially take care of everything, like HR matters, like dismissals.
vacations.
Also, I was doing the payroll for them, so I was responsible for putting together payroll bonuses, keeping track of their activity as well.
didn't actually have a system to keep track of that, so we're doing a lot of things on spreadsheets back in the day.

And so that's how I collected all the information, and then I had to also present to the client, so I was essentially putting together reports, like sometimes biweekly, monthly, quarterly, it's hard to actually give highlights of the activity and how much we've done for the client.
it was a lot going on in that role.
I had seven different verticals.
They called it verticals, but they were actually industries.
so every vertical actually had a point in context, so I had to keep all of them in the loop of what was going on.
Well, I was also, you know, a managing team and taking care of, you know, one-on-one, coaching, making sure they were doing their job and whatnot.
So that was a great opportunity learn a lot about not only about recruiting, but also about leadership.
And then after that one, I was recruited by one person who worked at a recruiting agency back in the day.
So I got a message from that person and then started like chatting about an opportunity to become a full-cycled recruiter for an agency.
And the payment was really good for what I was making because as a leader for a call center, I wasn't really making much.
And so that full-cycle opportunity seemed right because there was also another world of recruiting that I didn't know, which is basically handling the whole thing from scratch to the end.
The job at
as a leader or what we were doing for Career Builder wasn't really full-cycle.

We're doing like just the initial phase like doing resume screening and interviewing people so that's how I got I got into the full cycle recruitment phase and then really something that stood out was the fact that I have a an educational background in computer science and so that was like crucial for me to like being selected for technical roles.

So I started doing like full-cycle technical recruiting for quite some years and that has been pretty much six years until now, almost six years, close to six years, not really that close, five or a few months.
So yeah, that's what I've been doing.
I started like a

I would say mid-level recycled recruiter back in the days and I grew into seniority and more recently I've been doing I guess for the past years I've been essentially leading other recruiters like coaching them like helping them ramp up and also managing clients directly.
That's awesome and tell me little bit more like kind of where you are right now like why the change and what are you looking for right now?

Yeah, full transparency I so in December I've been five years with the company that I am with right now, Rockstar and in December I decided to make a pass you know as a contractor we didn't actually get too many days off so I decided it was about time for me to like make a path you know like

Like a very good pause and then kind of come back ready for another five years until I asked them for an unpaid month that was in December and so I Guess I didn't ask and we didn't talk about it But I was thinking I was you know going to be like January the second or whatever I was just going to look back in and start like working, but it seems like things are not Going well in terms of like the projects that they're getting lately, so They want to work with me actually I'm still I don't I don't have Access like I don't have slack access.
don't have their account anything because I guess it's part of the process, but But they're still like trying to get me a project to rejoin them.
I'm still like in communications Almost every week with them, but I am not really seeing much of a progress
Um, looks like, I mean, I'm, I'm completely grateful with them because I feel like they're trying really hard to get me another project.
Um, but it looks like just the way that the market is right now.
They're not getting a lot of work and, and so they really need to find a project that they can give me so that I can't rejoin them.
And the thing is that I'm not an easy recruiter position into a project.
Because I am kind of an outlier in terms of compensation.
Um, so it's, it's, it's been really hard for them to actually find a project that would essentially be profitable with me on it, you know?
Yeah, that makes sense.
And, um, what, what is your ideal, like scenario, like moving forward both in terms of like role and, and also like compensation?
Great question.
So I, so really.
I've been doing recruiting for quite some time.
So I kind of have the need of like evolving at some point into like leading more.
And when I mean leading, I don't mean like becoming a manager or anything like that.
know recruiting just by the nature of the role it was required for the person to be in constant hands-on support all the time.
So I don't mean to like step out of recruiting completely.

But I do want to be in a role where I can essentially like have more flexibility on the stuff that I can do, like building processes or working on strategy and making sure I talk to clients or I talk to, well, not clients in this case, but I talk to, I guess, hiring managers.
And I hear them, which is pretty much what I've been doing right now with Rocket Bar clients.
But I want to do it.
I think it would be really interesting to do it for a.
for an actual company in a direct role.
So when I talk to Molly and really how this came out, it was like I said hi to her on LinkedIn because there was a lot of time since we connected.
And so once we were like, she actually brought up the opportunity.
And when I look at the job description, like kind of clicked immediately.
And I was like, OK, this sounds a lot like what I'm actually looking for.
so, yeah, like I told her like, hey, I'm going to password, I'm going to pause with rocket power.
And I think this opportunity actually looks great.
So I wanted to learn more.
And here I am.
Yeah.
And so in terms of compensation, full transparency, I was making $9,000 a month with rocket power.
I understand that's what I call it, a glitch in the system for someone in London.

America, I know that's kind of, that's a big pay, but so like I told Molly, I know even if I say, and I think also that's a problem because they're really having a hard time finding me a project because of that.
And so I think that's okay.
don't pretend to actually get the same pay.
I know the industry has changed a lot and the market as well.
So I was telling Molly, I feel like if I could make 7K, that would be like, I'll be very happy with that.
But I also could be flexible.

Like if there's a lot of learning involved, if I know that job that's going to be like long-term and the company wants to work with me for a long-term while we grow together, I don't mind starting at a lower pay.
If the, I guess the other part, it's going to be
compensated with the learning piece on it.
Yeah, that makes sense.
I think like so, you know, obviously you have a great background.
I think the the main area that I would want to dig in is like a lot of our needs are outside of the technical side because our CTO is like pretty hands-on on the on the recruiting side.

There's definitely some benefits to fact that you know you have a CS degree and like you have a lot of experience with technical recruiting but I would say like long term where like 80 percent of our hires are going to be non-technical like you know the engineering hires are going to be very hands-on with with our CTO and you know like in some of the assessments he's going to put he puts together and things like that so there's still some like coordination there's some like vetting screening you know we're using a lot of like our own AI workflows to help with the first pass of screenings and you know but I think a lot of it is around like our managing editors finding a
writers, finding like people to with SEO backgrounds and like those types of roles, right?
So I'm just curious like for some of those where, you know, walk me through a little bit of your process, right?
we have a managing editor role right now, you know, kind of understand like, hey, let's say we we start with a small project or start with a project like, you know, Monday, right?

before for our managing editor role, which is open, like, well, understand a little bit of your your process of jumping in because I think we have a lot of immediate needs for the non-technical side as well.
Got it.

I'm not I wouldn't be at least myself I wouldn't be concerned about the about like it's not really changing industry honestly because I that tech side hasn't really doing it's not doing pretty well lately.
And for the most part on 2020 24 at Rocket Bar, I was doing actually tech.
Tech recruiting because again, there really aren't projects for tech right now.
So I was doing other stuff like I was doing like CTOs, CEOs, COOs for companies.
Just to give you a quick example, one of the projects I was working on was a holding company, holding company, it's the word.
So they essentially had like a ton of companies that they require in the industrial manufacturing space.
So I was required to essentially find COOs or CCOs for them as well, even CEOs for industrial companies.
So I wouldn't be concerned about it.
know I came to that pretty quickly to those kind of roles.
I know there's a lot of digging into how they

industry because I know it's not only about understanding the role, but also understanding the industry and what this people actually likes And how to attract the talent as well, but definitely not something I would be I would be afraid of.
Um, I think Every tiny recruiter out there.
It's probably going through the same right now because they're really as they're really isn't Much work on tech.
So they have to transition into something else.
Um, and I guess that's part of like being A solid recruiter like being able to adapt to whatever the work's come.
Yeah, whether where the work is Cool So like let's take just for example like the managing out of your role, right?

And I know you I don't expect you to have had time to look into uh, sort or even like our open roles, but Um, but the the TLDRs Um, these are folks with, uh, content and seo and growth background that have run content programs for for brain

and you know, whether on the agency side or working directly for the brands, can you walk me through a little bit of like, how would you approach a new project, like assuming that was like a new client or just a new project you got thrown in like, well, just walk me through a little bit of your process, I understand.
Yeah, 100%.
I think it like my next immediate step would be to like data around like what has been done so far.
Of course, like initially, like take a look at the job descriptions and whatnot.
It's like publicly publicly available.
But I also would connect with the person who's been who is acting as the hiring manager of the role and collect as much information as I can to understand the role.
Also, if there has been some work already done on that end, I would try to connect with the person or the recruiter and in our if Molly is doing that.
But I'm trying to get information.
of like how much she has done.

If there is like a tracker of the people she has reached out to to kind of get an understanding of what type of profile she's going after and see not only what the recruiter has done so far but also seem like if there has been any success with the hiring manager to just to start to kind of like start trying to like understand the profile and see what has been working out and what not I think I would if I get a chance to have a meeting with the hiring manager I would first collect all the information that it's available with recruiters or publicly and whatever be prepared for that call and have very specific questions of what would be missing because I also don't want the hiring manager to go over the whole thing again with another recruiter so I'll make sure that I collect all the information and then come to a hiring manager with like better specific questions once I add that I think it's all
It's all a matter of go finding the right profiles.
As you said, it sounds like the help is needed ASAP, so I'm sure I can essentially hit the ground running.
Just start finding profiles, and of course, there's a calibration process, but I don't really think that a managing director would be that complex.

I think I've done other more complex roles in my career, so I think it's a matter of understanding the role and making sure that we hit the ground running as soon as possible, but totally you understand your concern though, I know it's like my background.

What are some of the things as far as, especially with newer companies, as far as sourcing additional candidates, finding the right candidates, any playbooks that work well for you obviously, I know that's a lot of what you've done as well.
I'm sorry.
I just want to make sure I understand the question.
You're asking if I have any sort of like playbooks or if I've done that for our company?
Yeah, like so.
Okay, so we set up this new role.
got to hire you walk me through a little bit of your process.
Like, what are some things you usually will do in order to like find and source great candidates as well, you know, for that role?
If there's not a pipeline yet.

Oh, Yeah, like I said, collect that information, the requirements, making sure I lock what I call lock in the profile, making sure I am crystal clear of what the hiring manager is looking for, and then jumping into essentially I could do it really depends on the role.
But I'll say if it's a managing editor, probably there's a good chance that these people are going to be on LinkedIn.
So I would go to LinkedIn recruiter as my first choice.
But there's also like other, other, other angles that we could take eventually like LinkedIn recruiters not working well or as expected.
There's other routes that we could use.
Also, a best practice that I've done with new searches is that not all of the accounts has it, but if it's available, I would use it for sure.
I don't know if you're familiar with LinkedIn talent insights.
It's basically a new product that would allow you to map the talent.

So if you provide like the keywords, the job titles, locations and whatnot of what you're looking for, it will give you like a supply of candidates' where what companies have that type of talent at CERTA.
So that's a good source.
I think also finding companies that might be doing something similar to the company.
And I kind of use that a lot on everything that I do.
So I question everything.
Like, if I don't, I get a bit frustrated if I understand the why of things.
So that, I think, has been very crucial to be successful in things that I do.
Because it's like, I don't just stick to this is what you have to do and just follow what I'm telling you.
I try to question, OK, yeah, that's fine.
I get your direction, but I want to understand the why behind that.
Because it kind of have to make sense to me in my head.
So I feel like passionate about moving forward.
But I think if I'm doing something that doesn't make sense to me, probably I'm not going to do it with the same passion as if I'm very clear of why I'm doing this.
And if it doesn't make sense, I'll try to come up with the right way that makes
and that I feel strong about like having success on it.
Cool.
That's awesome.

Well, I know this was like kind of last minute like scheduling, you know, I think we're still kind of trying to understand a little bit of like what kind of help we need, definitely like understanding like your comp is above the range that we originally had, but then like we've got to think through like the role a little bit more.
If we go if we were to go higher and especially with the needs we we have, right?
But you know, it's been great connecting and we'll we'll get back to you.
But I know like you probably have a timeline and everything, you know, like right now, like back to back interviews today as well, not for this role for other roles.
But I think like over the weekend, I can kind of clear my head a little bit and then we'll get back to you.
Okay, just one quick question.
and I know you get a jump.
Is there another interview after this like is there in another step after this one or I should hear back with from you whether you guys want to move forward or not?

You will hear back from us whether we want to move forward or not like mostly because we haven't officially kicked off like the search and we're also like thinking through like different levels and different types of roles right and we're currently hiring a chief of staff and and so who we hire as a chief of staff can make a difference and what type of role we want to hire on the recruiting and talent side and but then also like we have to be you know mindful of like margins and costs and things like that you know so so it's like really kind of like there's a lot of connecting pieces here we're definitely like best really in need of help to vet the candidates we have in our pipeline and we have like over 3,000 people apply.

purchase a staff, you know, um, and so there's definitely like a need for that, but I think the biggest ball acts, the fact that no matter who we put in place, we still need to go invest the time to explain like what we do and kind of how we do it and all that.

And so, so it's like we're in this tricky position where, you know, we just got to put the right people in place, um, in order to kind of help even in the meantime, just kind of like plow some of these roles, you know, got it.
Okay, sounds good.
Um, well, thanks, thanks again for the time.
appreciate it.
Great, great chatting and we'll talk soon.
Talk soon.
Have a good one.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Explore Argelio Baca's background and potential fit for a recruiting role at GrowthX.](https://fathom.video/share/jJEc2HDtsNsyXszPU4AEtBo8zAoir1E3?tab=summary&timestamp=60.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [Argelio has 10+ years of recruiting experience, including technical and leadership roles](https://fathom.video/share/jJEc2HDtsNsyXszPU4AEtBo8zAoir1E3?tab=summary&timestamp=72.0)
  - [Currently seeking new opportunities after a break from his previous role at Rocket Bar](https://fathom.video/share/jJEc2HDtsNsyXszPU4AEtBo8zAoir1E3?tab=summary&timestamp=394.0)
  - [GrowthX's immediate needs are primarily non-technical roles (e.g., managing editors, writers)](https://fathom.video/share/jJEc2HDtsNsyXszPU4AEtBo8zAoir1E3?tab=summary&timestamp=742.0)
  - [Compensation expectations and role definition need further alignment](https://fathom.video/share/jJEc2HDtsNsyXszPU4AEtBo8zAoir1E3?tab=summary&timestamp=645.0)

## Topics

### Argelio's Professional Background

  - [10+ years in recruiting, started in call centers supporting Career Builder](https://fathom.video/share/jJEc2HDtsNsyXszPU4AEtBo8zAoir1E3?tab=summary&timestamp=80.0)
  - [Progressed from basic resume screening to full-cycle technical recruiting](https://fathom.video/share/jJEc2HDtsNsyXszPU4AEtBo8zAoir1E3?tab=summary&timestamp=109.0)
  - [Leadership experience: managed 25 recruiters, handled HR matters, payroll, and client reporting](https://fathom.video/share/jJEc2HDtsNsyXszPU4AEtBo8zAoir1E3?tab=summary&timestamp=156.0)
  - [Recent focus: leading/coaching recruiters and managing clients directly](https://fathom.video/share/jJEc2HDtsNsyXszPU4AEtBo8zAoir1E3?tab=summary&timestamp=360.0)
  - [Educational background in computer science, crucial for technical recruiting roles](https://fathom.video/share/jJEc2HDtsNsyXszPU4AEtBo8zAoir1E3?tab=summary&timestamp=305.0)

### Current Situation and Job Search

  - [Recently took an unpaid month off from Rocket Bar (previous employer of 5 years)](https://fathom.video/share/jJEc2HDtsNsyXszPU4AEtBo8zAoir1E3?tab=summary&timestamp=394.0)
  - [Not currently active due to project scarcity; still in communication with Rocket Bar](https://fathom.video/share/jJEc2HDtsNsyXszPU4AEtBo8zAoir1E3?tab=summary&timestamp=420.0)
  - [Seeking evolution into more leadership/strategic roles while maintaining hands-on recruiting](https://fathom.video/share/jJEc2HDtsNsyXszPU4AEtBo8zAoir1E3?tab=summary&timestamp=552.0)
  - [Previous compensation: $9,000/month; open to $7,000/month or flexible for long-term growth](https://fathom.video/share/jJEc2HDtsNsyXszPU4AEtBo8zAoir1E3?tab=summary&timestamp=645.0)

### GrowthX's Recruiting Needs

  - [80% of hires are non-technical (e.g., managing editors, writers, SEO specialists)](https://fathom.video/share/jJEc2HDtsNsyXszPU4AEtBo8zAoir1E3?tab=summary&timestamp=742.0)
  - [CTO handles technical recruiting; need support for non-technical roles](https://fathom.video/share/jJEc2HDtsNsyXszPU4AEtBo8zAoir1E3?tab=summary&timestamp=742.0)
  - [Immediate need: managing editor role with content, SEO, and growth background](https://fathom.video/share/jJEc2HDtsNsyXszPU4AEtBo8zAoir1E3?tab=summary&timestamp=795.0)
  - [Using AI workflows for initial candidate screening](https://fathom.video/share/jJEc2HDtsNsyXszPU4AEtBo8zAoir1E3?tab=summary&timestamp=742.0)

### Argelio's Recruiting Approach

  - [Collects comprehensive information on the role and previous recruitment efforts](https://fathom.video/share/jJEc2HDtsNsyXszPU4AEtBo8zAoir1E3?tab=summary&timestamp=988.0)
  - [Connects with hiring managers to understand specific needs and preferences](https://fathom.video/share/jJEc2HDtsNsyXszPU4AEtBo8zAoir1E3?tab=summary&timestamp=995.0)
  - [Utilizes LinkedIn Recruiter and LinkedIn Talent Insights for candidate sourcing](https://fathom.video/share/jJEc2HDtsNsyXszPU4AEtBo8zAoir1E3?tab=summary&timestamp=1197.0)
  - [Adapts to different industries and role types quickly](https://fathom.video/share/jJEc2HDtsNsyXszPU4AEtBo8zAoir1E3?tab=summary&timestamp=1167.0)
  - [Emphasizes understanding the "why" behind requirements to ensure passionate execution](https://fathom.video/share/jJEc2HDtsNsyXszPU4AEtBo8zAoir1E3?tab=summary&timestamp=1329.0)

### GrowthX's Current Hiring Situation

  - [Hiring a Chief of Staff, which may impact the recruiting role definition](https://fathom.video/share/jJEc2HDtsNsyXszPU4AEtBo8zAoir1E3?tab=summary&timestamp=1411.0)
  - [Large candidate pool (3,000+ applicants) for Chief of Staff position needs vetting](https://fathom.video/share/jJEc2HDtsNsyXszPU4AEtBo8zAoir1E3?tab=summary&timestamp=1500.0)
  - [Balancing immediate hiring needs with the time investment required for onboarding](https://fathom.video/share/jJEc2HDtsNsyXszPU4AEtBo8zAoir1E3?tab=summary&timestamp=1513.0)

## Next Steps

  - [Marcel to review the role requirements and budget considerations over the weekend](https://fathom.video/share/jJEc2HDtsNsyXszPU4AEtBo8zAoir1E3?tab=summary&timestamp=1388.0)
  - [GrowthX team to align on the specific recruiting role needed based on other hires (e.g., Chief of Staff)](https://fathom.video/share/jJEc2HDtsNsyXszPU4AEtBo8zAoir1E3?tab=summary&timestamp=1411.0)
  - [GrowthX to follow up with Argelio regarding next steps or decision on moving forward](https://fathom.video/share/jJEc2HDtsNsyXszPU4AEtBo8zAoir1E3?tab=summary&timestamp=1422.0)

action_items.description: Review Argelio's fit for role; decide on next steps; communicate decision to Argelio
